meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on project ideas. Learn how to architect SaaS pricing models using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-value client solutions fast.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, treat SaaS pricing as your foundational architecture. By designing a tiered pricing model first, you force yourself to define specific feature sets and user personas. This constraint-based approach turns abstract uncertainty into concrete, buildable product requirements that clients are willing to pay for immediately.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a freelancer's graveyard. You have the technical stack, the ambition, and the billable hours, but you're paralyzed by the 'what'—the elusive perfect SaaS idea that never seems to arrive. This cycle of procrastination kills momentum and drains your potential to scale.

But here is the industry secret: don't start with the idea; start with the business logic. By reverse-engineering a SaaS pricing model, you define the value proposition and feature gates before writing a single line of code. This tutorial shifts your mindset from 'searching for ideas' to 'architecting outcomes,' allowing you to ship professional, high-converting SaaS products for your clients with surgical precision.

steps.title: Defining the Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the core unit of value (e.g., requests, storage, users) to structure your SaaS tiers. This creates the skeleton for your entire database schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze a B2B SaaS pricing page and extract the core value metric. Propose a tiered structure for a project management tool that incentivizes upgrading as user usage scales.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a database schema based on a 3-tier SaaS pricing model with usage-based limits'

steps.title: Architecting the Feature Gate Logic
steps.description: Implement middleware that restricts access based on subscription tiers. This is the heart of your SaaS application security and monetization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust middleware pattern in TypeScript that checks user subscription status against required feature permissions before executing API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'refactor this API route to include a permission check decorator based on the user's stripe subscription plan'

steps.title: Integrating Payment Infrastructure
steps.description: Connect your logic to Stripe to ensure the pricing tiers are enforced in the real world. Automate the synchronization between your app and the payment provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe webhook handler that listens for subscription updates and updates the user's access level in the local database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install stripe && claude add stripe-webhook-handler.ts'

steps.title: Building the Pricing Dashboard
steps.description: Create a high-converting UI component that displays your tiers. Use psychological pricing principles to highlight the 'Pro' plan.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a responsive pricing table component using Tailwind CSS that emphasizes the middle tier using a 'Most Popular' badge and unique styling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ui 'create a React pricing component with 3 tiers and hover animations using Tailwind'

steps.title: Validating with Synthetic Usage
steps.description: Run simulations to ensure your pricing tiers don't break under load. Test the edge cases where a user exceeds their tier limits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite that simulates a user hitting their usage limit and verifying the system correctly returns a 403 Forbidden status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'run integration tests for subscription tier upgrades and usage limit enforcement'

pro_tips: Always price by the unit of value (e.g., per email sent) rather than per user to ensure your revenue grows alongside your client's success.
Use 'Shadow Tiers'—create an enterprise tier that is hidden until a user hits a specific high-usage threshold to capture high-ticket leads.
Never hardcode pricing logic; store your tier configurations in a JSON object in your database so you can adjust pricing without redeploying code.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your middleware to prevent immediate service disruption on failed credit card payments, which improves churn rates.
Use Claude Code to generate 'Negative Test Cases'—specifically ask it to break your pricing logic to find loopholes in your subscription gating.
content.next_steps: Once the infrastructure is live, deploy your pricing page to a staging environment, run a load test using your generated synthetic data, and present the modular architecture to your client as a 'Growth-Ready SaaS Framework'.
content.conversion_block: You now have a repeatable framework to turn any client requirement into a profitable SaaS engine. Stop struggling with the blank page and start building systems that actually scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
